parentQuestion: Parasympathetic stimulation of the heart causes ___.

scaffolds.q: The vagus nerve (CN X) provides parasympathetic innervation to the heart.
scaffolds.explain: The vagus nerve is the great wanderer of parasympathetic control — it reaches from brainstem to heart, lungs, and GI tract. Hold this thought:: for the heart, vagal fibers synapse in ganglia within the cardiac wall, and the short postganglionic fibers release ACh onto the SA and AV nodes. Absorb this:the calming influence.

scaffolds.q: The SA node is the natural pacemaker of the heart.
scaffolds.explain: The sinoatrial (SA) node fires spontaneously at about 100 bpm without any neural input. parasympathetic slows this below 100; sympathetic speeds it above 100. Your resting heart rate (~70 bpm) reflects parasympathetic dominance. The vagus is always gently braking the heart.
